channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Prison guards and inmates say New York's correctional system is broken
item.description: Prison guards in New York say the state's correctional system is in crisis. Both guards and inmates are pleading with the state to fix what they say is a broken system.
item.content:encoded: Prison guards in New York say the state's correctional system is in crisis. Both guards and inmates are pleading with the state to fix what they say is a broken system.
item.dc:creator: Emily Russell

item.title: Why the Stillaguamish Tribe in Washington is buying up farmland
item.description: The Stillaguamish Tribe north of Seattle is returning farmland to the sea to save salmon and help floodproof a community that's struggled with rising tides due to climate change.
item.content:encoded: The Stillaguamish Tribe north of Seattle is returning farmland to the sea to save salmon and help floodproof a community that's struggled with rising tides due to climate change.
item.dc:creator: John Ryan

item.title: This week in space news: Artemis II next steps and a mysterious interstellar visitor
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Regina Barber and Nell Greenfieldboyce about the Artemis moon missions, the "seismic quiet" during a solar eclipse, and the origins of a mysterious interstellar visitor.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Regina Barber and Nell Greenfieldboyce about the Artemis moon missions, the "seismic quiet" during a solar eclipse, and the origins of a mysterious interstellar visitor.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: A driver lifted a teen's spirits at a stoplight
item.description: Forty years ago, a 19-year-old woman sat behind the wheel of her car, sobbing and feeling like her life had fallen apart. A stranger pulled up beside her at a stoplight and lifted her spirits.
item.content:encoded: Forty years ago, a 19-year-old woman sat behind the wheel of her car, sobbing and feeling like her life had fallen apart. A stranger pulled up beside her at a stoplight and lifted her spirits.

item.title: Alleged gunman at White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court
item.description: The alleged gunman at the White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court Monday for his arraignment, where he faced charges of attempted assassination of the president, among others.
item.content:encoded: The alleged gunman at the White House Correspondents' Dinner appeared in court Monday for his arraignment, where he faced charges of attempted assassination of the president, among others.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Supreme Court heard case on how to label risks of popular weed killer
item.description: How the Supreme Court rules could have implications for tens of thousands of lawsuits against Roundup maker Monsanto, which is now owned by Bayer.
item.content:encoded:
How the Supreme Court rules could have implications for tens of thousands of lawsuits against Roundup maker Monsanto, which is now owned by Bayer.

(Image credit: Jean-Francois Monier)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Internet blackout causes huge damage to Iran economy
item.description: Iran has cut off the internet during the war, and the blackout is hurting businesses and the crumbling economy.
item.content:encoded: Iran has cut off the internet during the war, and the blackout is hurting businesses and the crumbling economy.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Privacy and law enforcement clash as the Supreme Court wrestles with 'geofence' warrants
item.description: In oral arguments at the Supreme Court Monday, most of the justices aimed pointed questions at both sides, with the usual conservative-liberal alignments scrambled like an egg.
item.content:encoded:
In oral arguments at the Supreme Court Monday, most of the justices aimed pointed questions at both sides, with the usual conservative-liberal alignments scrambled like an egg.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Could the World Cup turn into a bit of a bust?
item.description: Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are disillusioned with ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they are deciding not to come — raising concerns across the travel industry.
item.content:encoded: Some fans in the U.S. and around the world are disillusioned with ticket prices — and U.S. immigration policies. So they are deciding not to come — raising concerns across the travel industry.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: What baby teeth reveal about developing baby brains
item.description: A new study examined baby teeth and found there are critical windows early in a child's life when their developing brains are particularly vulnerable to exposures to metals in the environment.
item.content:encoded: A new study examined baby teeth and found there are critical windows early in a child's life when their developing brains are particularly vulnerable to exposures to metals in the environment.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy
